warnings: GRID: dimension chain matches EE-03 (same ground-floor base plan) and the master grid file exactly, including the 2.05m dummy-grid extension south of grid A.
This is a 2-story house so this sheet only covers the ground floor; the corresponding upper-floor outlet plan is EE-06 (page 64, separate file).

Total outlet count summed from this best-effort element list is 11 -- all share the same '+1.20M' standard mounting height. Per EE-01's legend, plain outlets in dry rooms are the ⊖ duplex type; none of these 11 appear to be in a wet area on this floor, so the waterproof ⊟ variant is not expected here (not independently confirmed which glyph variant each uses at this resolution, but the double-outlet clusters near the kitchen boundaries are clearly the standard ⊖ symbol drawn twice side-by-side, not the boxed ⊟ waterproof variant).

No outlet symbol was confidently identified in ซักล้าง, ครัว itself (only its boundary walls), ห้องน้ำ 1, เก็บของ, เฉลียง, ทางเดิน, or จอดรถ -- consistent with the pattern on house #4's equivalent EE-05 sheet (wet/utility/carport rooms commonly rely on adjacent-room circuits or have none).

The '3/LP' circuit tag near the staircase cross-validates against this house's own EE-02 panel schedule (page 60), circuit position 3 (RCBO 16AT,1P, load 'เต้ารับ') -- consistent with this being the ground-floor outlet circuit feed.

The two double-outlet clusters (near the ทานอาหาร/corridor boundary and near the เตรียมอาหาร/ครัว boundary) are read as 2 outlets each based on the visibly doubled circle glyph, not a single outlet with a duplicated label -- flagged for human review since the two overlapping circles are close enough together that miscounting is possible at this resolution.
